EN.title: Biometric authentication devices
EN.description: Fingerprint and facial recognition security solutions
EN.body:
## Overview

VaultWares biometric authentication devices provide passwordless security using fingerprint scanning and facial recognition technology. These devices integrate seamlessly with your existing infrastructure to add an additional layer of security through biometric verification.

## Product lineup

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="VaultScan Fingerprint" icon="fingerprint">
    **Type**: USB fingerprint scanner  
    **Resolution**: 500 DPI  
    **Certification**: FBI PIV-071006
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultScan Pro" icon="hand">
    **Type**: Multi-finger scanner  
    **Resolution**: 1000 DPI  
    **Certification**: FBI PIV-071006, FIPS 201
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultFace Recognition" icon="user-check">
    **Type**: 3D facial recognition camera  
    **Technology**: Infrared + RGB  
    **Certification**: ISO/IEC 30107-3
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultAccess Terminal" icon="tablet">
    **Type**: All-in-one access control terminal  
    **Features**: Fingerprint + face + card reader  
    **Display**: 7" touchscreen
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Key features

### Biometric capabilities
- **High accuracy**: False acceptance rate (FAR) < 0.001%
- **Fast authentication**: < 1 second verification time
- **Liveness detection**: Anti-spoofing technology prevents fake fingerprints/photos
- **Multi-modal**: Combine fingerprint and facial recognition for enhanced security
- **Large capacity**: Store up to 10,000 biometric templates per device

### Security features
- **Template encryption**: Biometric data encrypted with AES-256
- **Secure storage**: Templates stored in tamper-resistant memory
- **No raw data storage**: Only mathematical templates stored, not actual images
- **Privacy compliant**: GDPR and BIPA compliant biometric data handling
- **Audit logging**: Complete authentication attempt logs

### Integration capabilities
- **LDAP/Active Directory**: Sync with existing user directories
- **SAML/OAuth**: Integrate with SSO providers
- **REST API**: Programmatic enrollment and authentication
- **SDK support**: Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android
- **Physical access control**: Integrate with door locks and turnstiles

## Technical specifications

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="VaultScan Fingerprint">
    | Specification | Details |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Sensor type | Optical fingerprint sensor |
    | Resolution | 500 DPI |
    | Image size | 256 x 360 pixels |
    | Scan area | 15mm x 20mm |
    | Interface | USB 2.0 |
    | FAR | < 0.001% |
    | FRR | < 0.1% |
    | Verification time | < 0.5 seconds |
    | Template size | 512 bytes |
    | Operating temp | 0°C to 45°C |
    | Dimensions | 65mm x 45mm x 25mm |
    | Certification | FBI PIV-071006 |
    | Warranty | 3 years |
  </Tab>
  
  <Tab title="VaultScan Pro">
    | Specification | Details |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Sensor type | Capacitive multi-finger sensor |
    | Resolution | 1000 DPI |
    | Image size | 512 x 512 pixels |
    | Scan area | 25mm x 25mm |
    | Interface | USB 3.0 |
    | FAR | < 0.0001% |
    | FRR | < 0.05% |
    | Verification time | < 0.3 seconds |
    | Template size | 1024 bytes |
    | Operating temp | 0°C to 45°C |
    | Dimensions | 85mm x 65mm x 30mm |
    | Certification | FBI PIV-071006, FIPS 201 |
    | Warranty | 5 years |
  </Tab>
  
  <Tab title="VaultFace Recognition">
    | Specification | Details |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Camera type | 3D structured light |
    | Resolution | 1920 x 1080 RGB + IR |
    | Depth accuracy | ±1mm at 0.5m |
    | Recognition distance | 0.3m to 1.5m |
    | Interface | USB 3.0 |
    | FAR | < 0.0001% |
    | FRR | < 0.1% |
    | Verification time | < 0.8 seconds |
    | Liveness detection | Active (3D depth analysis) |
    | Operating temp | 0°C to 40°C |
    | Dimensions | 120mm x 40mm x 30mm |
    | Certification | ISO/IEC 30107-3 Level 2 |
    | Warranty | 3 years |
  </Tab>
  
  <Tab title="VaultAccess Terminal">
    | Specification | Details |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Display | 7" capacitive touchscreen (1024x600) |
    | Fingerprint | 500 DPI optical sensor |
    | Face recognition | 2MP RGB + IR camera |
    | Card reader | RFID (125kHz + 13.56MHz) |
    | Interface | Ethernet, Wi-Fi, USB |
    | Capacity | 10,000 users, 100,000 events |
    | Relay output | 2x relay for door/gate control |
    | Wiegand | Input/output support |
    | Power | 12V DC, 2A |
    | Operating temp | -10°C to 50°C |
    | Dimensions | 200mm x 150mm x 40mm |
    | Mounting | Wall-mount or desktop stand |
    | Warranty | 5 years |
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Setup and usage

### Initial setup

<Steps>
  <Step title="Connect the device">
    Plug the biometric device into an available USB port or connect via network (for terminal models).
  </Step>
  <Step title="Install software">
    ```bash
    # Download and install VaultWares Biometric Suite
    wget https://downloads.vaultwares.com/biometric-suite-latest.deb
    sudo dpkg -i biometric-suite-latest.deb
    ```
  </Step>
  <Step title="Configure device">
    ```bash
    # Initialize device
    vw-biometric init --device /dev/vaultscan0
    
    # Set security level
    vw-biometric config --security-level high --liveness-check enabled
    ```
  </Step>
  <Step title="Enroll users">
    Launch the enrollment wizard or use the CLI to register biometric templates.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### User enrollment

<CodeGroup>
```bash CLI enrollment
# Enroll fingerprint
vw-biometric enroll \
  --user john.doe \
  --type fingerprint \
  --finger right-index \
  --samples 3

# Enroll face
vw-biometric enroll \
  --user john.doe \
  --type face \
  --samples 5 \
  --require-liveness
```

```python Python SDK
from vaultwares import BiometricSDK

# Initialize SDK
sdk = BiometricSDK(device='/dev/vaultscan0')

# Enroll fingerprint
template = sdk.enroll_fingerprint(
    user_id='john.doe',
    finger='right_index',
    samples=3
)

# Save template
sdk.save_template(template, encrypted=True)
```

```javascript Node.js SDK
const { BiometricSDK } = require('vaultwares-biometric');

// Initialize SDK
const sdk = new BiometricSDK({ device: '/dev/vaultscan0' });

// Enroll fingerprint
const template = await sdk.enrollFingerprint({
  userId: 'john.doe',
  finger: 'right_index',
  samples: 3
});

// Save template
await sdk.saveTemplate(template, { encrypted: true });
```
</CodeGroup>

### Authentication

<CodeGroup>
```bash CLI authentication
# Verify fingerprint
vw-biometric verify \
  --type fingerprint \
  --timeout 10s

# Output:
# User: john.doe
# Match score: 98.7%
# Status: AUTHENTICATED
```

```python Python SDK
from vaultwares import BiometricSDK

sdk = BiometricSDK(device='/dev/vaultscan0')

# Capture and verify
result = sdk.verify_fingerprint(timeout=10)

if result.authenticated:
    print(f"User {result.user_id} authenticated")
    print(f"Match score: {result.score}%")
else:
    print("Authentication failed")
```

```javascript Node.js SDK
const { BiometricSDK } = require('vaultwares-biometric');

const sdk = new BiometricSDK({ device: '/dev/vaultscan0' });

// Capture and verify
const result = await sdk.verifyFingerprint({ timeout: 10000 });

if (result.authenticated) {
  console.log(`User ${result.userId} authenticated`);
  console.log(`Match score: ${result.score}%`);
} else {
  console.log('Authentication failed');
}
```
</CodeGroup>

## Integration examples

### Windows Hello integration

```powershell
# Register VaultWares as Windows Hello provider
Register-VaultWaresHelloProvider -DeviceType Fingerprint

# Configure policy
Set-VaultWaresHelloPolicy `
  -RequireBiometric $true `
  -AllowPIN $false `
  -RequireTPM $true
```

### Linux PAM integration

```bash
# Configure PAM for biometric authentication
sudo nano /etc/pam.d/common-auth

# Add before other auth methods:
auth    sufficient    pam_vaultwares.so
auth    required      pam_unix.so try_first_pass
```

### Active Directory integration

```bash
# Sync users from Active Directory
vw-biometric ad-sync \
  --server dc1.example.com \
  --domain example.com \
  --base-dn "OU=Users,DC=example,DC=com" \
  --sync-interval 1h

# Map AD users to biometric templates
vw-biometric ad-map \
  --attribute sAMAccountName \
  --auto-enroll false
```

### Web application integration

```javascript
// JavaScript Web API
const vaultBio = new VaultWaresBiometric({
  apiKey: 'your-api-key',
  endpoint: 'https://biometric.example.com'
});

// Request authentication
async function authenticateUser() {
  try {
    const result = await vaultBio.authenticate({
      type: 'fingerprint',
      timeout: 15000
    });
    
    if (result.success) {
      // User authenticated
      console.log('Welcome, ' + result.userId);
      // Proceed with login
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Authentication failed:', error);
  }
}
```

### Physical access control

```bash
# Configure door controller
vw-biometric access-control \
  --device terminal-01 \
  --relay 1 \
  --unlock-duration 5s \
  --require-biometric fingerprint \
  --require-card true

# Set access schedule
vw-biometric schedule \
  --device terminal-01 \
  --user john.doe \
  --days mon-fri \
  --hours 08:00-18:00
```

## Multi-factor authentication

### Combine biometric with other factors

```bash
# Configure MFA policy
vw-biometric mfa-policy \
  --require-biometric fingerprint \
  --require-pin true \
  --require-card true \
  --any-two-of-three

# User must provide 2 of 3 factors:
# 1. Fingerprint
# 2. PIN code
# 3. RFID card
```

### Adaptive authentication

```json
{
  "adaptive_policy": {
    "low_risk": {
      "factors": ["fingerprint"],
      "conditions": ["known_device", "known_location"]
    },
    "medium_risk": {
      "factors": ["fingerprint", "pin"],
      "conditions": ["new_device", "known_location"]
    },
    "high_risk": {
      "factors": ["fingerprint", "face", "pin"],
      "conditions": ["new_device", "unknown_location"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Privacy and compliance

### GDPR compliance

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Data minimization">
    VaultWares stores only mathematical templates, not raw biometric images. Templates cannot be reverse-engineered to recreate the original biometric data.
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="User consent">
    Built-in consent management requires explicit user approval before enrollment.
    ```bash
    vw-biometric enroll --require-consent --consent-text "custom-text.txt"
    ```
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="Right to erasure">
    Users can request deletion of their biometric data at any time.
    ```bash
    vw-biometric delete-user --user john.doe --purge-all-data
    ```
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="Data portability">
    Export user biometric templates in encrypted format.
    ```bash
    vw-biometric export --user john.doe --output user-data.enc
    ```
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

### BIPA compliance (Illinois)

- **Written consent**: Obtain written release before collecting biometric data
- **Retention policy**: Automatically delete templates after specified period
- **No selling**: Templates are never shared or sold to third parties
- **Security**: AES-256 encryption for all stored templates

```bash
# Configure BIPA-compliant retention
vw-biometric retention-policy \
  --max-age 3years \
  --auto-delete true \
  --require-written-consent
```

## Monitoring and reporting

### Authentication logs

```bash
# View authentication attempts
vw-biometric logs \
  --start-date "2024-01-01" \
  --end-date "2024-01-31" \
  --status all \
  --format csv > auth-report.csv

# Real-time monitoring
vw-biometric monitor --live
```

### Performance metrics

```bash
# Device health check
vw-biometric health-check

# Output:
# Device: VaultScan Pro (/dev/vaultscan0)
# Status: Online
# Sensor health: Good
# Template count: 1,247
# Avg verification time: 0.28s
# Success rate (24h): 99.2%
# Failed attempts (24h): 12
```

## Troubleshooting

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Fingerprint not recognized">
    - Clean the sensor with a soft, dry cloth
    - Ensure finger is dry and clean
    - Try different finger pressure
    - Re-enroll the fingerprint with more samples
    - Check sensor health: `vw-biometric health-check`
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="Face recognition fails">
    - Ensure adequate lighting (not too bright or dark)
    - Remove glasses or accessories if causing issues
    - Position face within 0.3-1.5m range
    - Look directly at camera
    - Re-enroll with different lighting conditions
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="High false rejection rate">
    - Lower security threshold: `vw-biometric config --threshold medium`
    - Enroll more samples per user
    - Check for sensor wear or damage
    - Update firmware: `vw-biometric update-firmware`
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="Device not detected">
    - Check USB connection
    - Verify drivers installed: `vw-biometric-driver --version`
    - Check device permissions: `ls -l /dev/vaultscan*`
    - Restart biometric service: `sudo systemctl restart vaultwares-biometric`
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Next steps

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Installation guide" icon="download" href="/installation/hardware-installation">
    Detailed setup instructions
  </Card>
  <Card title="API reference" icon="code" href="/api-reference/endpoints">
    Programmatic integration
  </Card>
  <Card title="Best practices" icon="star" href="/guides/best-practices">
    Security recommendations
  </Card>
  <Card title="Support" icon="headset" href="/support/contact">
    Get technical assistance
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

QC.title: Dispositifs d'authentification biométrique
QC.description: Solutions de sécurité par empreintes digitales et reconnaissance faciale
QC.body:
## Aperçu

Les dispositifs d'authentification biométrique VaultWares offrent une sécurité sans mot de passe grâce à la technologie de scan d'empreintes digitales et de reconnaissance faciale. Ces appareils s'intègrent parfaitement à votre infrastructure existante pour ajouter une couche de sécurité supplémentaire par vérification biométrique.

## Gamme de produits

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="VaultScan Fingerprint" icon="fingerprint">
    **Type**: Scanner d'empreintes digitales USB
    **Résolution**: 500 DPI
    **Certification**: FBI PIV-071006
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultScan Pro" icon="hand">
    **Type**: Scanner multi-doigts
    **Résolution**: 1000 DPI
    **Certification**: FBI PIV-071006, FIPS 201
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultFace Recognition" icon="user-check">
    **Type**: Caméra de reconnaissance faciale 3D
    **Technologie**: Infrarouge + RGB
    **Certification**: ISO/IEC 30107-3
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultAccess Terminal" icon="tablet">
    **Type**: Terminal de contrôle d'accès tout-en-un
    **Fonctionnalités**: Empreinte digitale + visage + lecteur de carte
    **Écran**: Écran tactile 7 pouces
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Fonctionnalités clés

### Capacités biométriques
- **Haute précision**: Taux de fausse acceptation (FAR) < 0,001%
- **Authentification rapide**: Temps de vérification < 1 seconde
- **Détection de vivacité**: La technologie anti-spoofing empêche les fausses empreintes/photos
- **Multi-modal**: Combinez empreinte digitale et reconnaissance faciale pour une sécurité accrue
- **Grande capacité**: Stocke jusqu'à 10 000 modèles biométriques par appareil

### Fonctionnalités de sécurité
- **Chiffrement des modèles**: Données biométriques chiffrées avec AES-256
- **Stockage sécurisé**: Les modèles sont stockés dans une mémoire résistante aux altérations
- **Pas de stockage de données brutes**: Seuls les modèles mathématiques sont stockés, pas les images réelles
- **Conforme à la confidentialité**: Gestion des données biométriques conforme au RGPD et au BIPA
- **Journalisation des audits**: Journaux complets des tentatives d'authentification

### Capacités d'intégration
- **LDAP/Active Directory**: Synchronisation avec les annuaires utilisateurs existants
- **SAML/OAuth**: Intégration avec les fournisseurs SSO
- **API REST**: Enrôlement et authentification programmatiques
- **Support SDK**: Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android
- **Contrôle d'accès physique**: Intégration avec les serrures de porte et les tourniquets

## Spécifications techniques

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="VaultScan Fingerprint">
    | Spécification | Détails |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Type de capteur | Capteur d'empreintes digitales optique |
    | Résolution | 500 DPI |
    | Taille de l'image | 256 x 360 pixels |
    | Zone de scan | 15mm x 20mm |
    | Interface | USB 2.0 |
    | FAR | < 0,001% |
    | FRR | < 0,1% |
    | Temps de vérification | < 0,5 secondes |
    | Taille du modèle | 512 octets |
    | Temp. de fonctionnement | 0°C à 45°C |
    | Dimensions | 65mm x 45mm x 25mm |
    | Certification | FBI PIV-071006 |
    | Garantie | 3 ans |
  </Tab>

  <Tab title="VaultScan Pro">
    | Spécification | Détails |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Type de capteur | Capteur multi-doigts capacitif |
    | Résolution | 1000 DPI |
    | Taille de l'image | 512 x 512 pixels |
    | Zone de scan | 25mm x 25mm |
    | Interface | USB 3.0 |
    | FAR | < 0,0001% |
    | FRR | < 0,05% |
    | Temps de vérification | < 0,3 secondes |
    | Taille du modèle | 1024 octets |
    | Temp. de fonctionnement | 0°C à 45°C |
    | Dimensions | 85mm x 65mm x 30mm |
    | Certification | FBI PIV-071006, FIPS 201 |
    | Garantie | 5 ans |
  </Tab>

  <Tab title="VaultFace Recognition">
    | Spécification | Détails |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Type de caméra | Lumière structurée 3D |
    | Résolution | 1920 x 1080 RGB + IR |
    | Précision de profondeur | ±1mm à 0,5m |
    | Distance de reconnaissance | 0,3m à 1,5m |
    | Interface | USB 3.0 |
    | FAR | < 0,0001% |
    | FRR | < 0,1% |
    | Temps de vérification | < 0,8 secondes |
    | Détection de vivacité | Active (analyse de profondeur 3D) |
    | Temp. de fonctionnement | 0°C à 40°C |
    | Dimensions | 120mm x 40mm x 30mm |
    | Certification | ISO/IEC 30107-3 Niveau 2 |
    | Garantie | 3 ans |
  </Tab>

  <Tab title="VaultAccess Terminal">
    | Spécification | Détails |
    |--------------|---------|
    | Écran | Écran tactile capacitif 7" (1024x600) |
    | Empreinte digitale | Capteur optique 500 DPI |
    | Reconnaissance faciale | Caméra 2MP RGB + IR |
    | Lecteur de carte | RFID (125kHz + 13.56MHz) |
    | Interface | Ethernet, Wi-Fi, USB |
    | Capacité | 10 000 utilisateurs, 100 000 événements |
    | Sortie de relais | 2x relais pour contrôle de porte/barrière |
    | Wiegand | Support d'entrée/sortie |
    | Alimentation | 12V CC, 2A |
    | Temp. de fonctionnement | -10°C à 50°C |
    | Dimensions | 200mm x 150mm x 40mm |
    | Montage | Mural ou support de bureau |
    | Garantie | 5 ans |
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Installation et utilisation

### Configuration initiale

<Steps>
  <Step title="Connecter l'appareil">
    Branchez l'appareil biométrique sur un port USB disponible ou connectez-le via le réseau (pour les modèles de terminal).
  </Step>
  <Step title="Installer le logiciel">
    ```bash
    # Download and install VaultWares Biometric Suite
    wget https://downloads.vaultwares.com/biometric-suite-latest.deb
    sudo dpkg -i biometric-suite-latest.deb
    ```
  </Step>
  <Step title="Configurer l'appareil">
    ```bash
    # Initialize device
    vw-biometric init --device /dev/vaultscan0

    # Set security level
    vw-biometric config --security-level high --liveness-check enabled
    ```
  </Step>
  <Step title="Enrôler les utilisateurs">
    Lancez l'assistant d'enrôlement ou utilisez la CLI pour enregistrer les modèles biométriques.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### Enrôlement utilisateur

<CodeGroup>
```bash CLI enrollment
# Enroll fingerprint
vw-biometric enroll \
  --user john.doe \
  --type fingerprint \
  --finger right-index \
  --samples 3

# Enroll face
vw-biometric enroll \
  --user john.doe \
  --type face \
  --samples 5 \
  --require-liveness
```

```python Python SDK
from vaultwares import BiometricSDK

# Initialize SDK
sdk = BiometricSDK(device='/dev/vaultscan0')

# Enroll fingerprint
template = sdk.enroll_fingerprint(
    user_id='john.doe',
    finger='right_index',
    samples=3
)

# Save template
sdk.save_template(template, encrypted=True)
```

```javascript Node.js SDK
const { BiometricSDK } = require('vaultwares-biometric');

// Initialize SDK
const sdk = new BiometricSDK({ device: '/dev/vaultscan0' });

// Enroll fingerprint
const template = await sdk.enrollFingerprint({
  userId: 'john.doe',
  finger: 'right_index',
  samples: 3
});

// Save template
await sdk.saveTemplate(template, { encrypted: true });
```
</CodeGroup>

### Authentification

<CodeGroup>
```bash CLI authentication
# Verify fingerprint
vw-biometric verify \
  --type fingerprint \
  --timeout 10s

# Output:
# User: john.doe
# Match score: 98.7%
# Status: AUTHENTICATED
```

```python Python SDK
from vaultwares import BiometricSDK

sdk = BiometricSDK(device='/dev/vaultscan0')

# Capture and verify
result = sdk.verify_fingerprint(timeout=10)

if result.authenticated:
    print(f"User {result.user_id} authenticated")
    print(f"Match score: {result.score}%")
else:
    print("Authentication failed")
```

```javascript Node.js SDK
const { BiometricSDK } = require('vaultwares-biometric');

const sdk = new BiometricSDK({ device: '/dev/vaultscan0' });

// Capture and verify
const result = await sdk.verifyFingerprint({ timeout: 10000 });

if (result.authenticated) {
  console.log(`User ${result.userId} authenticated`);
  console.log(`Match score: ${result.score}%`);
} else {
  console.log('Authentication failed');
}
```
</CodeGroup>

## Exemples d'intégration

### Intégration Windows Hello

```powershell
# Register VaultWares as Windows Hello provider
Register-VaultWaresHelloProvider -DeviceType Fingerprint

# Configure policy
Set-VaultWaresHelloPolicy `
  -RequireBiometric $true `
  -AllowPIN $false `
  -RequireTPM $true
```

### Intégration Linux PAM

```bash
# Configure PAM for biometric authentication
sudo nano /etc/pam.d/common-auth

# Add before other auth methods:
auth    sufficient    pam_vaultwares.so
auth    required      pam_unix.so try_first_pass
```

### Intégration Active Directory

```bash
# Sync users from Active Directory
vw-biometric ad-sync \
  --server dc1.example.com \
  --domain example.com \
  --base-dn "OU=Users,DC=example,DC=com" \
  --sync-interval 1h

# Map AD users to biometric templates
vw-biometric ad-map \
  --attribute sAMAccountName \
  --auto-enroll false
```

### Intégration d'applications web

```javascript
// JavaScript Web API
const vaultBio = new VaultWaresBiometric({
  apiKey: 'your-api-key',
  endpoint: 'https://biometric.example.com'
});

// Request authentication
async function authenticateUser() {
  try {
    const result = await vaultBio.authenticate({
      type: 'fingerprint',
      timeout: 15000
    });

    if (result.success) {
      // User authenticated
      console.log('Welcome, ' + result.userId);
      // Proceed with login
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Authentication failed:', error);
  }
}
```

### Contrôle d'accès physique

```bash
# Configure door controller
vw-biometric access-control \
  --device terminal-01 \
  --relay 1 \
  --unlock-duration 5s \
  --require-biometric fingerprint \
  --require-card true

# Set access schedule
vw-biometric schedule \
  --device terminal-01 \
  --user john.doe \
  --days mon-fri \
  --hours 08:00-18:00
```

## Authentification multi-facteurs

### Combiner biométrie avec d'autres facteurs

```bash
# Configure MFA policy
vw-biometric mfa-policy \
  --require-biometric fingerprint \
  --require-pin true \
  --require-card true \
  --any-two-of-three

# User must provide 2 of 3 factors:
# 1. Fingerprint
# 2. PIN code
# 3. RFID card
```

### Authentification adaptative

```json
{
  "adaptive_policy": {
    "low_risk": {
      "factors": ["fingerprint"],
      "conditions": ["known_device", "known_location"]
    },
    "medium_risk": {
      "factors": ["fingerprint", "pin"],
      "conditions": ["new_device", "known_location"]
    },
    "high_risk": {
      "factors": ["fingerprint", "face", "pin"],
      "conditions": ["new_device", "unknown_location"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Confidentialité et conformité

### Conformité RGPD

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Minimisation des données">
    VaultWares ne stocke que des modèles mathématiques, pas des images biométriques brutes. Les modèles ne peuvent pas être rétro-ingénierisés pour recréer les données biométriques originales.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Consentement de l'utilisateur">
    La gestion du consentement intégrée exige l'approbation explicite de l'utilisateur avant l'enrôlement.
    ```bash
    vw-biometric enroll --require-consent --consent-text "custom-text.txt"
    ```
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Droit à l'effacement">
    Les utilisateurs peuvent demander la suppression de leurs données biométriques à tout moment.
    ```bash
    vw-biometric delete-user --user john.doe --purge-all-data
    ```
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Portabilité des données">
    Exportez les modèles biométriques des utilisateurs dans un format chiffré.
    ```bash
    vw-biometric export --user john.doe --output user-data.enc
    ```
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

### Conformité BIPA (Illinois)

- **Consentement écrit**: Obtenir une décharge écrite avant de collecter des données biométriques
- **Politique de rétention**: Suppression automatique des modèles après une période spécifiée
- **Pas de vente**: Les modèles ne sont jamais partagés ou vendus à des tiers
- **Sécurité**: Chiffrement AES-256 pour tous les modèles stockés

```bash
# Configure BIPA-compliant retention
vw-biometric retention-policy \
  --max-age 3years \
  --auto-delete true \
  --require-written-consent
```

## Surveillance et rapports

### Journaux d'authentification

```bash
# View authentication attempts
vw-biometric logs \
  --start-date "2024-01-01" \
  --end-date "2024-01-31" \
  --status all \
  --format csv > auth-report.csv

# Real-time monitoring
vw-biometric monitor --live
```

### Métriques de performance

```bash
# Device health check
vw-biometric health-check

# Output:
# Device: VaultScan Pro (/dev/vaultscan0)
# Status: Online
# Sensor health: Good
# Template count: 1,247
# Avg verification time: 0.28s
# Success rate (24h): 99.2%
# Failed attempts (24h): 12
```

## Dépannage

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Empreinte digitale non reconnue">
    - Nettoyez le capteur avec un chiffon doux et sec
    - Assurez-vous que le doigt est sec et propre
    - Essayez différentes pressions du doigt
    - Ré-enrôlez l'empreinte digitale avec plus d'échantillons
    - Vérifiez l'état du capteur : `vw-biometric health-check`
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Échec de la reconnaissance faciale">
    - Assurez un éclairage adéquat (ni trop vif ni trop sombre)
    - Retirez les lunettes ou accessoires si cela cause des problèmes
    - Positionnez le visage dans la plage de 0,3 à 1,5m
    - Regardez directement la caméra
    - Ré-enrôlez dans différentes conditions d'éclairage
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Taux de rejet élevé">
    - Abaisser le seuil de sécurité : `vw-biometric config --threshold medium`
    - Enrôler plus d'échantillons par utilisateur
    - Vérifier l'usure ou les dommages du capteur
    - Mettre à jour le micrologiciel : `vw-biometric update-firmware`
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Appareil non détecté">
    - Vérifiez la connexion USB
    - Vérifiez que les pilotes sont installés : `vw-biometric-driver --version`
    - Vérifiez les permissions de l'appareil : `ls -l /dev/vaultscan*`
    - Redémarrer le service biométrique : `sudo systemctl restart vaultwares-biometric`
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>
